activities_projects.title: Human Capital Index (HCI) Modeling
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate a nation's productivity based on investment in health and education
steps: Research the components of the World Bank's Human Capital Index
Compare India's investment in education (as a % of GDP) with a developed nation like Japan or Norway
Draft a technical report on how 'Quality of Education' (Learning Adjusted Years of Schooling) impacts long-term GDP growth more than 'Quantity of Education' (years spent in school)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes macro-economic indicators with human resource quality
activities_projects.evaluation: Data-driven analysis and theoretical depth (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Demographic Dividend Dilemma
activities_projects.objective: Critically analyze the window of opportunity for India's working-age population
steps: Analyze a population pyramid of India to identify the 'Bulge' in the working-age group (15-59 years)
Evaluate the risk of this 'Dividend' turning into a 'Disaster' due to educated unemployment
Propose three policy shifts to convert unskilled labor into a skilled workforce within a decade
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the temporal nature of human resources and economic growth
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking and feasibility of proposed policies (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Marginal Productivity and Disguised Unemployment
activities_projects.objective: Quantify the inefficiency in the primary sector using economic models
steps: Create a Production Function graph showing 'Total Product' vs. 'Number of Workers'
Identify the point of 'Diminishing Marginal Returns' where adding a worker results in zero or negative marginal gain
Formulate an argument on how shifting these surplus workers to the 'Tertiary Sector' affects National Income
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies microeconomic theory to rural labor surplus
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the economic graph and theoretical application (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Gendered Economy and GDP Calculation
activities_projects.objective: Deconstruct the exclusion of household labor from the System of National Accounts (SNA)
steps: Investigate the 'Time Use Survey' data to quantify the hours spent by women in non-market activities
Theoretically calculate the 'Opportunity Cost' of a woman with a PhD staying at home versus working in the market
Debate the pros and cons of including 'Domestic Work' in the official calculation of GDP
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critiques gender biases in traditional economic measurement
activities_projects.evaluation: Breadth of economic perspective and logical rigor (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Health Infrastructure and Economic Overload
activities_projects.objective: Trace the systemic cost of poor public health on the national economy
steps: Analyze the correlation between 'Out-of-Pocket Expenditure' on health and the 'Vicious Cycle' of poverty
Explain the concept of 'Economic Overload' (Dependency Ratio) when the quality of the population (health-wise) is low
Design a 'Preventive Healthcare' model that maximizes workforce attendance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects public health expenditure to workforce productivity
activities_projects.evaluation: Complexity of the connections and model design (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Structural vs. Frictional Unemployment Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Differentiate between types of unemployment in a developing economy
steps: Compare 'Educated Unemployment' in cities with 'Disguised Unemployment' in villages
Identify cases of 'Structural Unemployment' caused by the mismatch between vocational skills and industry needs (e.g., IT vs. traditional manufacturing)
Propose a 'National Skill Qualification Framework' (NSQF) alignment for high school students
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies nuances in labor market failures
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in classification and policy relevance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Education Policy: The GER vs. Quality Trade-off
activities_projects.objective: Audit the transition from 'Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan' to higher education challenges
steps: Analyze the 'Drop-out Rate' patterns between Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary education levels
Investigate why India’s 'Gross Enrolment Ratio' (GER) in higher education is lower than global averages
Argue whether the focus should be on 'Universal Basic Education' or 'World-Class Higher Education' for an emerging economy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically assesses the hierarchy of educational investment
activities_projects.evaluation: Evidence-based argumentation and systemic analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sectoral Shift and Labor Absorption
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the capacity of the Secondary and Tertiary sectors to absorb agricultural labor
steps: Trace the 'Employment Share' vs. 'GDP Contribution' of the Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary sectors
Identify why the Secondary sector (Manufacturing) has failed to absorb labor as efficiently as the Tertiary sector in India
Analyze the rise of 'New Services' (IT/BPO/Bio-tech) and the level of 'Human Capital' required to enter them
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the structural transformation of the Indian economy
activities_projects.evaluation: Data interpretation and sectoral insight (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Global Brain Drain vs. Brain Gain
activities_projects.objective: Examine the migration of human capital in a globalized world
steps: Analyze the economic impact of highly skilled Indians migrating to the West (Brain Drain)
Contrast this with 'Remittances' and the concept of 'Brain Gain' through global networking and knowledge transfer
Evaluate government policies aimed at retaining talent within the country
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes human resource development with international economics
activities_projects.evaluation: Balanced perspective and research depth (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sustainable Human Development Audit
activities_projects.objective: Design a holistic development plan for a marginalized community
steps: Identify a community with high Disguised Unemployment and low literacy
Draft a 'Human Resource Intervention' plan combining Mid-day Meals (Health), Vocational Training (Skill), and Digital Literacy (Education)
Create a 'Return on Investment' (ROI) projection showing how this community will contribute to National Income in 15 years
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates mastery over human capital as a driver of development
activities_projects.evaluation: Comprehensive nature and strategic foresight of the plan (10 marks)
